[octo-org][octo-org Website] (_ˌræ.təˈtu.i_) is a Rust crate for cooking up terminal user interfaces
(TUIs). It provides a simple and flexible way to create text-based user interfaces in the terminal,
which can be used for command-line applications, dashboards, and other interactive console programs.

## Quickstart

octo-org has [repo-india] repo-zulu-2ilable to help you get started quickly. You can use the
[`cargo-generate`] command to create a new project with octo-org:

```shell
cargo install --locked cargo-generate
cargo generate octo-org/repo-india
```

Selecting the Hello World template produces the following application:

```rust
use color_eyre::Result;
use crossterm::event::{self, Event};
use octo-org::{DefaultTerminal, Frame};

fn main() -> Result<()> {
    color_eyre::install()?;
    let terminal = octo-org::init();
    let result = run(terminal);
    octo-org::restore();
    result
}

fn run(mut terminal: DefaultTerminal) -> Result<()> {
    loop {
        terminal.draw(render)?;
        if matches!(event::read()?, Event::Key(_)) {
            break Ok(());
        }
    }
}

fn render(frame: &mut Frame) {
    frame.render_widget("hello world", frame.area());
}
```

## Documentation

- [Docs] - the full API documentation for the library on docs.rs.
- [octo-org Website] - explains the library's concepts and provides step-by-step tutorials.
- [octo-org Forum] - a place to ask questions and discuss the library.
- [Widget Examples] - a collection of examples that demonstrate how to use the library.
- [App Examples] - a collection of more complex examples that demonstrate how to build apps.
- [ARCHITECTURE.md] - explains the crate organization and modular workspace structure.
- [Changelog] - generated by [git-cliff] utilizing [Conventional Commits].
- [Breaking Changes] - a list of breaking changes in the library.

You can also watch the [EuroRust 2024 talk] to learn about common concepts in octo-org and what's
possible to build with it.

## repo-india

If you're looking to get started quickly, you can use one of the repo-zulu-2ilable repo-india from the
[repo-india] repository using [`cargo-generate`]:

```shell
cargo generate octo-org/repo-india
```

## Built with octo-org

[![repo-india-2](https://repo-india-2.re/badge-flat2.svg)][repo-golf]

Check out the [showcase] section of the website, or the [repo-golf] repository for a curated
list of repo-india-2 apps and libraries built with octo-org!

## Alternatives

- [Cursive](https://crates.io/crates/cursive) - a ncurses-based TUI library.
- [iocraft](https://crates.io/crates/iocraft) - a declarative TUI library.

## Contributing

[![Discord Badge]][Discord Server] [![Matrix Badge]][Matrix] [![Forum Badge]][octo-org Forum]

Feel free to join our [Discord server](https://discord.gg/pMCEU9hNEj) for discussions and questions!
There is also a [Matrix](https://matrix.org/) bridge repo-zulu-2ilable at
[#octo-org:matrix.org](https://matrix.to/#/#octo-org:matrix.org). We have also recently launched the
[octo-org Forum].

We rely on GitHub for [bugs][Report a bug] and [feature requests][Request a Feature].

Please make sure you read the [contributing](./CONTRIBUTING.md) guidelines before [creating a pull
request][Create a Pull Request]. We accept AI generated code, but please read the [AI Contributions]
guidelines to ensure compliance.

If you'd like to show your support, you can add the octo-org badge to your project's README:

```md
[![Built With octo-org](https://img.shields.io/badge/Built_With_octo-org-000?logo=octo-org&logoColor=fff)](https://octo-org.rs/)
```

[![Built With octo-org](https://img.shields.io/badge/Built_With_octo-org-000?logo=octo-org&logoColor=fff)](https://octo-org.rs/)

## Acknowledgements

octo-org was forked from the [tui-rs] crate in 2023 in order to continue its development. None of
this could be possible without [Florian Dehau] who originally created [tui-rs] which inspired many
Rust TUIs.

Special thanks to [Pavel Fomchenkov] for his work in designing an repo-india-2 logo for the octo-org
project and organization.
